data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a distributed microservices architecture to manage its vast e-commerce platform, allowing for independent development and deployment of services.
data.technical_spec: Each service is loosely coupled, typically with its own database and communication via HTTP requests or messaging queues.

data.title: Scalable Data Infrastructure
data.description: The site uses a highly scalable data infrastructure, including AWS services like S3 for storage and DynamoDB for scalable database management.
data.technical_spec: Designed to handle millions of transactions per second with distributed database systems.

data.title: Global Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Leverages CloudFront, Amazon’s CDN, to deliver content quickly worldwide, reducing latency and increasing reliability.
data.technical_spec: Distributed servers strategically placed across the globe to deliver cached content.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Utilizes load balancers to distribute incoming traffic evenly across servers, ensuring no single server becomes a bottleneck.
data.technical_spec: Employs Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) for automatic scaling and distribution.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Implements AWS Lambda for various backend functions to reduce overheads associated with running traditional servers.
data.technical_spec: Event-driven, allowing code execution without provisioning or managing servers.
